title: descurainia pinnata subsp. filipes
intro: Descurainia pinnata subsp. filipes, also known as the western wallflower, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is native to the western United States and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including arthritis, eczema, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Descurainia pinnata subsp. filipes typically involve the use of its leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to promote overall health and well-being.
